user_query:
Please set up a small, local weekly automation that I can schedule to run every Monday morning to prep a concise rehearsal brief for my partner (who leads a music team). I admire their dedication to music and their faith, but I want the tone of anything I send to stay supportive and secular.

Inputs (all relative to the workspace):
- input/rehearsal_schedule.csv — Fields: date (YYYY-MM-DD), start_time (HH:MM, 24h), duration_minutes (int), location (string), next (true/false), songs (titles separated by |). Use the single row where next=true as the target rehearsal.
- input/songs_catalog.json — Array of objects {"title": string, "introduced": YYYY-MM-DD, "is_new": true/false}. Use this to compute how many songs in the next rehearsal are marked is_new=true.
- input/design_workload.csv — Fields: task, date (YYYY-MM-DD), start_time (HH:MM), end_time (HH:MM), client. Use this to detect scheduling conflicts with the rehearsal window.
- input/notes.md — Existing personal notes that include a "## Next rehearsal" section. This section is outdated and should be rewritten in-place based on the new summary, while preserving all other sections.
- input/email_template.txt — A plain-text template with placeholders like {{rehearsal_date}}, {{start_time}}, {{end_time}}, {{location}}, {{total_songs}}, {{new_songs_count}}, {{song_list}}, {{conflicts_summary}}, {{sender_name}}.

What to build and run now:
1) Create a single, runnable script at automation/weekly_brief (you may choose the extension, e.g., .py or .sh) that:
   - Reads input/rehearsal_schedule.csv, selects the row where next=true, and derives: rehearsal_date, start_time, end_time (start + duration_minutes), location, and the full song_list (split on | and sorted alphabetically).
   - Reads input/songs_catalog.json and computes new_songs_count for the selected rehearsal by counting songs from song_list where is_new=true in the catalog.
   - Reads input/design_workload.csv and determines any conflicts. A conflict exists if time intervals overlap (rehearsal_start < task_end AND task_start < rehearsal_end) on the same date. Include all overlapping tasks (with their time ranges and client) in a concise conflicts_summary; if none, set "None noted.".
   - Composes a brief weekly status report (secular tone) with the fields: rehearsal_date, start_time–end_time, location, total_songs, new_songs_count, and a bullet list of songs; end with a conflict note line derived from conflicts_summary.
   - Fills input/email_template.txt placeholders with the same values and sender_name="Avery" to produce a draft email. The first line must be a subject line in the form: "Subject: Weekly rehearsal check-in — {rehearsal_date}". Keep the tone supportive without religious phrasing.
   - Rewrites only the content of the "## Next rehearsal" section in input/notes.md to reflect the new data (date, time range, location, total songs, new songs count, and a short sentence noting any conflicts). Preserve all other headings/sections and their content.
   - Writes outputs to the exact paths below.

2) Produce the following artifacts:
- output/status_report.md — The weekly report containing the structured summary and conflicts note.
- output/draft_email.txt — The subject line and email body produced from the template and data above (secular, supportive tone, sender_name set to "Avery").
- output/schedule/run_weekly.sh — A small wrapper that invokes automation/weekly_brief from the repository root and writes a one-line timestamped message to output/logs/last_run.txt after completion (create the logs directory if needed).
- output/schedule/weekly_cron.txt — A single crontab line that would run the wrapper every Monday at 09:00 (local time), assuming bash is available and paths are relative to the repo root (use: 0 9 * * MON bash output/schedule/run_weekly.sh).
- Updated input/notes.md — Only the "## Next rehearsal" section replaced; other content unchanged.

3) Run the automation once now using your script so the outputs above are actually generated with the provided inputs.

Output constraints and checks:
- new_songs_count must equal the number of titles in the selected song_list whose is_new=true in input/songs_catalog.json.
- total_songs equals the count of titles in the selected song_list.
- Conflicts detection uses inclusive-overlap logic: rehearsal_start < task_end AND task_start < rehearsal_end (same date). List all overlaps in conflicts_summary in the email/report.
- The status report and the rewritten notes section must reflect the same counts and details (date, time range, location, total_songs, new_songs_count).
- Do not fetch or require any Internet resources.

Input paths:
- input/rehearsal_schedule.csv
- input/songs_catalog.json
- input/design_workload.csv
- input/notes.md
- input/email_template.txt

Output paths to create:
- output/status_report.md
- output/draft_email.txt
- output/schedule/run_weekly.sh
- output/schedule/weekly_cron.txt
- output/logs/last_run.txt (written by the wrapper)
- input/notes.md (updated in place)

I’ll wire the cron entry later; for now, please make sure the script and wrapper work from the repo root and that the outputs above are present after you run it once. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
